title: Is Vegetable-spring-rolls Gluten Free? | BiteRight
description: Public gluten safety analysis for Vegetable-spring-rolls. See major risks, safer alternatives, and what to ask before ordering.
heading: Is Vegetable-spring-rolls gluten free?
intro: This public analysis report explains the biggest gluten risks in Vegetable-spring-rolls and how to order more safely.

verdict.summary: Vegetable spring rolls may contain gluten due to the use of wheat-based wrappers and potential cross-contamination during preparation. It is important to verify the specific ingredients and cooking methods used.

disclaimer: This guidance is informational only. Always verify ingredients and preparation with the restaurant.

sections.title: Quick answer
sections.body: Vegetable-spring-rolls can vary by recipe, ingredients, and cross-contact controls in the kitchen.

sections.title: Common gluten risks
sections.body: Watch for hidden sources like soy sauce, malt flavoring, marinades, thickeners, and shared fryers.

sections.title: How BiteRight helps
sections.body: Scan menus and ingredient labels with BiteRight to get a localized gluten-risk breakdown in seconds.

waiter_script.preview: Are the spring roll wrappers made from rice flour or wheat flour?

faq.question: Can BiteRight confirm if Vegetable-spring-rolls is gluten free?
faq.answer: BiteRight highlights likely gluten risks based on ingredients and preparation. Always confirm with the kitchen if you have coeliac disease.

faq.question: What should I ask a restaurant?
faq.answer: Ask about shared equipment, sauces, marinades, and whether the kitchen has a dedicated gluten-free prep area.
